review_text: We stayed at the park but really just a parking spot.  There are no trees and the trailers.are.quite close together.  Has all the amenities we needed but it is nothing special.  Note that they do have large groups of kids at times.  Very noisy when we got there but by 9 pm all was quiet.
Facilities are first class. Huge slab sites catering for the biggest rigs. Certainly not the cheapest place but one of the best laid out parks.

I wish I could give more than 5 stars for this park, most notably for the service.
The park itself is very good, the grounds are sensibly laid out and the ammenities are clean and modern. No issues there.

I want to give a big shoutout to Nat especially. We were using this park as our base for our time in Canberra, touring with a camper trailer.
Unfortunately, a freak storm burst damaged our camper and wrecked parts of our set up making it difficult to spend the night in the camper.
Natalie was able to get us sorted with some temporary accommodation and helped us with lots of practical elements and flexibility while we worked out a plan forward.

Would highly recommend this camp because of this service.

Additionally, if you're a school, church, or group booking, they have modern and spacious facilities to accommodate literally hundreds of people.

Thanks to Nat and the team at Discovery Parks Canberra.

Dave and fam
Friendly and helpful staff!

We stayed there for 8 days over Easter to explore Canberra, the amenities are clean and well looked after. The power sites are large and quite flat with concert slab.
The camp kitchen is spaces with plenty of indoor bbqs.
I would suggest they do need to get a few more outdoor bbqs around the park and the should look into getting a playground and pool to keep everyone entertained while they are there.
Highlight of our stay was the Easter breakfast and games they supplied for the family this was very well organised and catered for amazing effort goes out to the management and team members who helped put this on.

We had a drive through site. Very tidy grounds, we only used the laundry facilities which were clean and tidy. Very handy you can pay not using coins.

Would happily stay here again :)
Easy drive through sites, quality and clean amenities. Easy access to and from city

Good park, smallish.  New facilities.  Could use some trees, but what there are will grow. On the pricey side compared to other parks in region.
Use the Epic show ground if available for a cheaper rate.

What a great park! Well maintained. Staff were friendly and helpful. Clean facilities with rainfall showers and plenty of hot water - that stayed hot!!!
Drive Through site with concrete slab was level and heaps of space and not too close to neighbours. Camp kitchen was clean and spacious. The Park is situated close to the attractions and shops. There were days when it was a bit windy but not overly so. Thoroughly enjoyed our stay!

We have stayed at Canberra Park twice now  and the staff have been amazing. They have gone out of there way to help us with our many odd requests and changes in plans and they are always hepful and kind. The camp itself  is clean and tidy with an amazing camp kitchen and laundry. The showers even have shampoo supplied. We don't have children so not having a playground and pool wasn't an issue for us. Also if you stay there you have to try out Chris's coffee from his on-site Brew-On-The-Move truck.
Thanks again.
John Payne
You cannot get a better place to stay in Canberra, while on a school camp. Running like a Swiss clock!
Lovely large concrete sites, new clean amenities, very quiet area but not far to drive to the city.  Would highly recommend 😊
Good amenities. Handy for getting into Canberra, or the showground.

New park makes for very modern, clean facilities.  The laundry is exceptional.  Easy drive through slabs, not too close to neighbours. Garden and trees are a work in progress.  Second time here, won't be the last👍
Great place to stay with a caravan. Clean and well organised. Close to all you need to see in Canberra.

Clean and modern amenities, it’s not the prettiest of parks but it is clean and well maintained. Great for an overnight stay or like us stay an extra night or two and take in the amazing sites and restaurants in Canberra which is a short drive away. Dog friendly
Great location for event we attend annually

This place is basically a truck stop-over. The gardens and grass are/is dead, weeds, long - not maintained at all - in fact in our 4 nights at the property, we did not see one staff member outside of the office ha. Plenty of bindies and prickles as well. The bins were overflowing as well. Charging top dollar for a place with nothing for kids - no playground, no nothing! Our other issue is that we arrived (having booked 2 months in advance) to be told that our site had been taken by someone else and that she had to find us another available site??? Like um what? We had a reservation. Goodness me.
Loos had no paper first thing in morning

absolute peakness came here for skl camp and it was js so peak only bad thing is we ate chicken every day of the week which wasnt the best but it was good

Beds are squeaky as hell pls bring WD40 if u stay in the rooms
Bathrooms and showers are rlly clean and the beds r comfy.
PLS FIX THE SQUEAKYNESS ITS SO BAD LIKE BRO
We found it affordable, facilities very clean.concrete slabs are good size for your van. Staff are very helpful, friendly and good in letting you know the must see places.

New and large park
Caravan sites awesome with good drive through ones
I asked admin for a little extra help for my mum who has mnd and they were so helpful putting us right near facilities
A little out of town but Canberra easy to navigate so don’t stress
A lot of people will say but there is no pool playgrounds or trees true but it does not detract from what was such an easy place to stay
Well organized caravan park, loved  our stay here.

Why do they not plant any threes? The place lacks any personality and feels dull. The toilets, showers and camp kitchen were good though. If you want to visit the city it is close, but you need to take the car since the distances in Canberra are very large between different tourist attractions.

A fairly new place.
The caravan park section is really well laid out with lots of concrete drive through sites and concrete slab sites.
Lovely amenities and always clean.
Quiet place.
Easy to get to all parts of Canberra from here.
Havent looked at what other accom is here.
A very well laid out park.
Seem to have caravan storage here as well.
Dogs allowed on leash.
Clean tidy rooms

Good location in Canberra. Still more for horsey people, meaning the surrounds are more for gymkhana type events and facilities are suited to a get showered etc and out to do eventing as quick as possible. Still a nice place to stay when nothing is on.

We ended up in Canberra because of a medical emergency that saw a family member in Canberra Hospital in a critical condition.
My sister (and wife of the person in hospital) booked this caravan park for an unknown length of time so that she still had some of “her space” in their caravan.
Let’s start with the actual park, I’d rate it 10/10 with excellent concrete slab sites, impeccable ammenities when it comes to cleanliness and location, great camp kitchen and a great location in the city.
The staff, particularly Liz, have gone way above what any business would normally do to help a guest. On hearing of my sister’s situation, without hesitation under Liz’s lead they arranged fresh cooked meals, homemade slices, a perfect site location and a feeling of genuine care that’s often missed. We felt comfortable leaving my sister In Canberra while we returned to Melbourne knowing she was in great care with a network of staff in the park only too happy to assist her in anyway needed.
I can not speak more highly of Liz and the team and would urge anyone staying in Canberra to stay at Discovery - Canberra Park.

The park is devoid of any personality. It's very open and we overlooked a recycling yard just unattractive. Being so open the winds howled through with gusts up to 90 plus km hr one day, but around 50 or 60 km hr other days. The wind was relentless through the day but eased off during the night. Apparently September to November can be very windy in Canberra and I wouldn't recommend a visit during these months. 🤷 The roads in are wide and it's easy to get in and out of the park. We had a drive through site which was easy to get on but if you have a powered site I think the roads are a bit narrow reversing in. The amenities were clean but not enough for the size of the park. A big camp kitchen. There's no pools playgrounds or games rooms.
The park was quiet.
The staff who we only met on our arrival were lovely and helpful.
Great spot,long drive through sites. Definitely recommend.
I’ve been there once before with my year five class for a Canberra excursion
It was HORRIBLE MY FRIEND GOT FOOD POISIONING AND THREW UP 6 OR 7 TIMES IT WAS AWFULLLLLLLLLLLL THE SAUSAGES WERE HARD THE HASHBROWNS WERE MOULDY THE CHICKEN WAS RAW NEVER COME HEREEEEEEEEEE
There was a bug in my salad

Stayed in a drive thru slab site, had an amazing stay. Close enough to town but in a quiet area. Top notch facilities, bathrooms are immaculate, nice big shower stalls. Camp kitchen is also spacious with heaps of bench space, ovens and cooktops. Gets breezy, but where doesn’t! Awesome place to leave the van and travel around and see the sights of Canberra! Perfect spot to watch the sunsets. Will definitely be back next time we are in Canberra, thanks guys!

Nice big caravan park with lots of space between sites.
No pool, minimal trees.
Very new & well equipped camp kitchen

Booked a site for my caravan. Was issued a small tent site. Got my money back but disappointed, but happy I didn't stay there. Young girl working in reception was lovely. Others there were hard to deal with and tried to make me feel stupid.
The look of the park. Go to a drive-in theatre and park your van there. Thats what you get for your money. Ugly horrible place would never go back there.
And a big thank you to Capital Country Caravan Park for placing us at the last minute of business on a Saturday

Definitely needs some signs out the front saying its discovery caravan park reallly not until we saw check in bay did we know we were at right place. Sites big enough but  tight getting onto the slab sites. Lacks facilities for families no playgrounds pool . Infrastructure new but overall like being in a carpark for caravans . Good location for spending your days sightseeing.

Stayed 12th - 16th august
Very quiet and large park. Terrific sites with concrete and grass sites. Very friendly staff and immaculate toilets and showers. There is a huge dormitory for students that they are well away from the Caravans and couldn’t hear a thing!
I am a student that went here and all I've got to say is, it was horrible and i would go never again.

I wouldn't call it fancy or anything. The rooms are clean other than the sheets have a few stains. The food is edible and the chicken was surprisingly good. We were defiantly not the ones who accidently bent the bunk bed in room B-13. he he har har. Doors for the toilet and Shower have no lock as well.
We used the caravan park which was very good. Fairly new so all nice.
Although location wise not being the best, Canberra park is truly amazing, clean tidy rooms, great staff, good catering, they have it all.

Great park, nice drive through sites.

Very efficient check-in
This was a great park to stay overnight in Canberra. No bells or whistles ( or landscaping) but very easy drive through sites and clean amenities
I'm a student and I now have food poisoning from the butter chicken, and the tenders were PINKA in side now my hole school is sick

me and my friends had a great time at this hotel.. but there was some problems. First off, I'm gonna start with the rooms, let me just say.. I fell off the bed, bad experience. second the food, the food.. made us drop like flys, we all vomited about 6-7 times in the same day, I don't reccomened this place.

New park, sites flat with large concrete slabs. Can get drive thru sites, but we didnt and our street very tight to reverse but the other streets looked fine (we were just opposite drive thru sites with cars parked right to edge of street). New amenities, which are fantastic apart from the high shower heads that make showering without wetting your hair challenging (take a shower cap in winter), but shower spacious with good seats and plenty of hooks and hot water. Would be nice to see a few shade trees planted as we imagine it gets pretty hot in summer and there is no shade.
This is overall a good caravan park but I've taken off stars because the lights in the bathroom went off while I was having a shower. At night

Came here for our school camp. Accommodation was decent tho the bed frame were damaged and creaked everytime you moved. The mattress were fortunately clean and so was the rest of the room. The food was good but not great. I wouldn't mind coming again for a personal trip.
It was great! The ammenities were new and easy. The food was great some days, overall enjoyed my stay. The beds were a little creaky.
Small blankets

Stayed at Canberra park, for four nights in our caravan as we did the tourist rounds, we found it to be a convenient location for this purpose, the staff were welcoming and the park is well looked after with large van sites, clean amenities and a large camp kitchen conveniently enclosed for those cold Canberra nights.

We enjoyed our stay in Canberra. The caravan park is in a great location to base yourself from to visit all the attractions Canberra has to offer. We left our caravan in the park while we went to stay at the Jamala wildlife park for two nights and felt that our van was very secure. The staff were very friendly and facilitates were great. We definitely stay again when we are in Canberra.

The staff with a nose piercing EXTREMELY rude! Told the kids that if they yelled in the night they would be put in time out. TIME OUT? WHAT? THAT ONE STAFF MEMBER FIRE HER!

THE FOOD WAS ALSO DISGUSTING ABSOLUTELY DISGUSTING! I VOMITED LIKE 7 TIMES! AND THE DOOR WOULDN’T SHUT! DONT STAY HERE IF YOU WANT A GOOD EXPERIENCE!
Great location, well kept and great service
Large concrete drive through sites, clean amenities  short drive to shops and the staff were friendly

We parked here with our caravan pitches are mainly concrete and drive through with some grassed area
Amenities are excellent and the park feels like it very new

excellent park very generous sites all offer both slab and grass which was great as we have a little dog and we put up a fenced pen
The amenities were lovely and clean and the laundry had heaps of both washer and dryer with easy pay with card method.
The location is perfect within an easy drive to all the attractions.
And the staff were awersome as they hosted a delicious breakfast for mother’s day to all us campers/caravaners
Great location, clean & quiet park 😀 will stay again
Clean, new, great facilities. Drive through sites. Quiet, well priced and convenient. Excellent amenities and well priced.

Great clean park with modern amenities! Friendly staff and great drive through sites, lost a star as the toilet/shower block was to cold making showers a bit unpleasant and the toilet roll holder is to low and my leg hits it.
Seems to have clean amenities and good slab. Like everything these days bit expensive.

This is a great place to stay!
This is the third time we have stayed here. We just stayed four nights on a powered site over the  Easter weekend. The park is very well run with great amenities. Our drive-through  site was level and concreted. The amenities block is fairly new and very clean. There is guest Wi-fi available.
The staff went that bit extra to make everyone's Easter a memorable one. Easter egg hunt for the littlies and hot cross buns.
We shall return!
Best place to stay when going to Summernats 👍🏻
It's a fairly new caravan park that has been designed well with good sized drive through sites. It is in a very easy to access location when coming from the Hume highway and along the Barton highway.

Bit disappointed.  I stayed here a few years ago.  It was a top park with a top price. The only thing now is the top price.  It has gone down hill. I will try to get in contact with Discovery  to inform them. But l wouldn't hold my breath.

Großer, praktischer Campingplatz mit Zufahrt durch Schranke. Duschen und Toiletten ausreichend vorhanden. Allerdings kein Spielplatz o.Ä., aber auch keine Bäume (Schatten). Das WLAN reicht auch nicht bis zum hinteren Ende des Platzes.
Great park, great location, friendly staff, great facilities, amenities and camp kitchen, close to public transport (light rail), will definitely be coming back.

A good caravan park located close to the city. There is a tram station 1.4km away that takes you right into the city centre and you can take your bikes on it. There is a caravan park is fairly new and really needs some trees planted to provide shade and screening from the afternoon sun. One annoying thing was the light in the female ammenities was on too short a timer and kept leaving my wife in the dark whilst showering.

We stayed here on a powered site with the caravan. The sites are all very well spaced and there is plenty of room. We had site 5 and I think we had the best place in the park. Ammenities are great and the camp kitchen is excellent. The staff are great and helpful.
Lovely park. Will be great when the trees grow.
I want to say I haven't even stayed there yet, and Chris has done me a solid. Thanks mate, looking forward to the stay
Very clean and quiet. Staff very friendly and helpful

All the staff at Canberra park are very friendly and accommodating. I reported that my car leaked engine oil on the drive through concrete surface to see what I should do to remove the oil. I was treated with respect and two staff members removed the oil stains. The amenities are very new and clean. We enjoyed our 4 day stay and would come again.
Did not stay.
Excellent

The staff at Canberra park were very friendly and accommodating. One of the staff members helped us out over the phone to get a spot in the already busy caravan park, we are very thankful! The amenities were very new and clean. It has everything you need and even a big indoor seating area in the camp kitchen. Really enjoyed our stay and would come again!
So clean and quiet, a very convenient location for visiting Canberra.

Nice spot to camp that's within walking distance (1.2km) to light rail into the city.  Very clean.  No shade, and main bathrooms closed for too long (90 minutes) daily for cleaning.  Otherwise, nice spot.
Nice clean park good size sights will stay again
Good park needs a pool sites are big. Shops not to far away for supplies can cycle there.

Extremely grumpy owner
Complaining about kids with water balloons there’s no where to cool off or get out of the sun no shaded area
very rude staff, wanted to google our next stop after our stay outside the park and were asked to leave the premises immediately without understanding our situation.

everything about this camp site was disgusting. horrible. I've never seen a place so disgusting there was police camping outside the building because of how bad the food and place was. i saw my friends drop like dominoes as they smelled the modly aroma of the "food" this were the kind of meals you would feed to prisoners in the 18th century. i saw my life flash before my eyes. this was hell. living hell. never come here please. if you do get trapped here, try escape while you can before its too late

Amazing caravan sites, big concrete slabs for van and anex plus room for the car.
Staff were excellent and friendly.
Amenities were modern and clean.
Our only criticism would be the lack of trees or shrubs, it was so sterile and lacked character.

Everything is very good here - nearly 1st class. Amenities clean in great number. Ambiente is to improve... No trees, only big clean sites.
Polite reception staff!
Fees 58$ 2 Adults, powered site.

Awesome industrial camp kitchen with eveything you could want. Fire pit is great too.
Happy staff and Friendly welcome.
Sites are concrete.
Always stay here when we go to Canberra in our van.
Stayed three nights in a drive thru site. No complaints would stay again.
Noice
Well this is a big park very new amenities are 5 star  slab sights all modern amenities well worth the stay
Safe, very walkable.  Can walk to Canberra Saturday morning markets.  Well maintained with friendly staff

Canberra park wasn't pleasant in my opinion. By the way I am talking about the school camp, not the caravan park.

The location was ok but not great. Not too far from the city centre but not very central.

The food wasn't great. It was edible - not raw, nor containing any nails or hair. While the waffles were amazing, food such as the bolognese and hashbrown was dry and very average. The food was mostly mediocre overall.

The dorm rooms were bad. The beds were squeaking every time I moved a bit. A few of the screws were loose. The bed on one of the bunks were bent at a significant angle. But the sheets were OK and the bed was a reasonable size.

The toilet and shower doors didn't have a lock. There was not much of a way of stopping the water from exiting the shower area, only a little ramp, which was annoying when my clothes fell off the hook and onto the wet floor inside the shower. The bathroom was overall quite clean though.

A few more issues with the dorm room includes our one and only keycard has to be outside our dorm door at all times which is annoying because anyone from our group could just open the door and walk into our room at any time and/or take the keycard off the hook (luckily those didn't happen to me). Also there wasn't much room for our four suitcases to be in the room comfortably. We kinda had to squeeze them into spots so that we can have a walkway. It would've been worse if we had a messy room mate.

The foyer outside of our dorms were spacious but had limited seating and we could only go inside the foyer with a teacher which could be annoying waiting for one out in the cold. Also the air-conditioning was good.

In conclusion I would not recommend staying here if you want to have a pleasant experience. The food is average, the location is OK and the dorms are bad.
Camp grounds are extensive and provide good facilities for a reasonable price

Great caravan park. It is big and spacious and the sites are unequalled!! The majority are proper drive through, all concrete including annexe area, each with its own power, water & sullage. It is secure with boom gate entry, and the camp kitchen is beyond 5 star, it is incredible actually.
One other reviewer said this is the park others parks should aspire to be.....I couldn't agree more.

The food was raw and then I found a hair in my breakfast for dinner I had chicken it was raw and desert I found a finger nail how disappointing do not let your schools be there because when me and student went there there was bed bug

I stayed here for school camp, and I got to say, the camp kitchen food was great. Nothing undercooked, raw or insipid.
The rooms were fine, I stayed in Quoll, and the rooms were okay. I got a pillow, a blanket and a bed sheet. The shower’s water pressure was inadequate, and the bunk bed I slept in was tremendously squeaky. The lock on the door of the shower didn’t work, as I tested it from the outside and when you turn the handle, the pin unlocked. But the food definitely brightened me up. Great job to the kitchen staff. In conclusion this place is reasonable for a short stay.
Great Van park to stay when visiting Canberra . Large sites, modern clean amenities with lovely hot showers. Amazing camp kitchen. And very close to city and tourist sites.

Without a doubt the cleanest ,well maintained caravan Park we have ever been to.The bathrooms gee you could eat your dinner of the floor and the laundry room was the same.We strongly recommend that you visit the caravan Park.

Terrific spot to park the van for a stay in our nations capital....close to town
Very clean and tidy.

The best service you could ask for. My daughter was on school camp and I couldn’t get a hold of our teachers. The caretaker went out of his way after hours to get a hold of our school group who was offsite at the time to ensure the teachers called me back. He even called me back to advise they would be calling and to stay by my phone. Although I have never stayed, I can guarantee it would be 5 stars based on my experience alone. Thank you so much!

This is the best caravan place in Canberra by a long way.  Fantastic facilities, slab parking, easy to access and close to the city.  The camp kitchen is so good it is almost beyond words.  It has everything you need or want
Excellent.

Wow what a great park. The slab sites are huge. Park was very clean and the amenities were brilliant.
Close to everything in Canberra.
Highly recommended!

I went here and my bathroom door unhinged and wouldn’t shut also a blueberry muffin from here gave me a migraine headaches that made me vomit 3 times and pasta sauce had heaps of oil in it as well as snot and other mysterious stains on my blanket that failed to keep me warm at night. Btw don’t go to Canberra unless you have to there’s nothing fun there and if you have to go don’t stay here.

We stayed here for a few weeks a couple of years ago. It was all 5 star. This year we just needed a place to store our van for a couple of weeks while we visited family. Management was so helpful and accommodating.

Fantastic park with huge drive through sites and clean amenities and  well set up camp kitchen. Re people commenting on lack of trees, we feel this is a good thing and makes parking easier and your van and tow vehicle clean. Huge thanks to Chris and all staff who actually take pride in the place. Well done and ta for Mothers Day brekky.
Convenient to Canberra CBD and they have very modern amenities which are kept spotless.  Also have very good WiFi 😃
Loved this park So easy to park the van, brand new amenities and a fantastic camp kitchen I know there’s not much in the way of trees but it is a new park and the set up more than makes up for it.
Great clean park. Good amenities, large sites. Obviously used by those passing through or visiting Canberra.

The park is pretty new, so very sparse with vegetation! Wind whips through there, so be prepared. Clean amenities & good that u can take dogs there. A couple of recommendations; a couple of laundry baskets 🧺 in the laundry room would b very welcome & 3 times during our stay I went into the 1st female toilet & it had run out of toilet paper 🧻, so it is obviously the most used toilet & could do with a double roll holder to avoid this happening!
Washing machines are card only $5/load. An enjoyable stay with neat sites.

Sehr schöner und sauberer Campingplatz. Leider war der Fußweg zur Straßenbahn etwas weit, dafür fährt die Bahn recht oft und bis in den späten Abend hinein. Tagestickets kosten 9.60, eine einfache Fahrt kostet 5 Dollar.
Was ich nicht verstanden habe: offene Mülltonnen in der Camp Kitchen - ?! Kein Wunder, dass es dort Ameisen 🐜 gibt. Aber insgesamt ein empfehlenswerter Campingplatz :-)

We stayed at Canberra Park with 25 other A'vans from the A'van Club of Australia. This park is exceptional, the drive through sites and amenities first class. The Club dinner we had in the Homestead was beautifully organised, with excellent food.
The only thing the park is lacking is trees, as there is lots of concrete but no shady trees, or outside covered areas.

Stayed 4 nights at Canberra Park. We know where to stay if we go back. Although lack of trees/shade, it won't change the service. Kitchen amenities were very modern and had large ovens, microwaves and all the other modern cons to help you cook. Has a book swap as well.

Super ausgestatteter Caravan Park, sehr saubere Einrichtungen wie Küche, Toiletten etc.
Zuvorkommendes, freundliches und hilfsbereites Team.
Flat slabs and no shade trees.

We had a powered site for two weeks over Christmas. The amenities are new and luxurious, the camp kitchen was amazing and the internet was fast. As we are travelling for 13 months, we brought the business Her Soul Temple, Ayurveda with us and they allowed us to set up the treatment tent and see clients here. We were so grateful for the amazing stay in this peaceful, quiet well kept park.
Stayed 5 days, very clean amenities staff friendly and helpful.  Camp kitchen best ever seen.
This was the ideal place to stay for what we were doing in Canberra

This is a very nice park. It has room accommodation, van sites ( including drive through ones) and the facilities are kept extremely clean. The staff are extremely friendly and helpful. Will 100% stay here again. Also a big thankyou to Chris and his wife at the park, they were very friendly to talk to and extremely helpful. But a generally a huge thankyou to all we dealt with.

Would be good if there were small trees between sites to give some shade. Otherwise the service was excellent, the amenities was superb. Clean and tidy. The kitchen was amazing.
Large open field next door for kids and dogs to enjoy. Location is also great being not far from shopping and dining.
We even got a tote bag upon arrival which contained information about events, shopping and dining in Canberra. Really happy with our stay. Highly recommended and would definitely stay again!

Really nice modern park
Facilities excellent
Staff really good
Highly recommend
Stayed at the Caravan park and it is 10/10. Best caravan park I have stayed at

We have a motorhome,great place to stay, the best camp kitchen l have ever seen, very clean,even soap supplied.
Very close to spend a few days looking  around Canberra.
Would recommend 10/10 for me.
What a great park, easy to park van,  nice clean faclities ,nice people,great location

Very clean amenities and roomy parking for caravan.
Concrete slabs
10 minutes to city.

The park has accom for (read somewhere) up to 400 in groups. While we stayed in van 8 bus loads arrived & last night 9. It has 40 rooms for group leaders & sep accom for bus drivers. They have a meals room with chef & conference rooms.
There are about 73 sites for vans, all lined up like a caravan sales yard, most on concrete slabs. The vans are next to open acreage leaving no respite from weather. If it's wet, you'll cop it. If it's hot, there are no trees or pool. Even in outback Renner Spring & Wauchope homesteads there were beautiful new pools. There is nowhere in the office to grab that bread you forgot up the street or to buy an icecream. There is a lovely camp kitchen with outdoor dining area, but the outdoor area has no roof, again, open to the elements. The amenities as you would expect were modern &  with the shower head 10' off the ground, you could be forgiven for thinking you would have to run between the drops to get wet. We have moved south. Already someone asked "did you stay in the concrete jungle?" There are no bbq's scattered around or seating under trees, oh forgot, no trees. If you stay, ask for a site at the bottom of the park because of the noise of buses departing/arriving & overkill of lighting in park. This is a lovely park to park your van while you sightsee but has no caravan park atmosphere & offers nothing. met a few people at clothes line who remarked not returning. In 18 months living in our van this is the most badly designed new park. Upon check in, staff were lovely but we never saw anyone else. Wouldn't say don't stay, it's up to you.

Beautifully layed out, great location to explore Canberra, clean and so well organised. The only criticism would be lack of shade, just needs some trees, thankfully the weather wasn't too hot for our stay.

We stayed here for 7 nights to explore Canberra and surrounds.
Can't praise the caravan park more.
Clean, well looked after grounds, easy access to sites, exceptional amenities for cooking and eating, under cover and air conditioned (essential as it was very cold).
Showers and toilets well kept, clean and tidy. One question though, why are the shower heads 10 feet up?
Very easy to get to most venues, shopping center about 5 min away.
One afternoon we had a busload of kids playing cricket on the oval, great to see kids having a great time, didn't know they were there apart from that.
Enjoyed our stay, will be back.

Wonderful young CP well maintained, modern and very clean and yes I agree with others that more trees are needed but other than that I stayed a couple of days and couldn't fault it, loved the easy check in as well 9/10
Caravan park is nice and tidy

We stayed here for a week and found the park great for size of sites, all new and drive thru fantastic. Amenities great and camp kitchen was has everything thought of. Check in was easy. Great location to everywhere you need to go. Only thing being new was no trees, but if planted around perimeter to stop some wind would be fantastic in time to come as a bit open and windy.
Caravan parking is the best park we have been too really recommended it
Very clean and tidy

Before we arrived, I received a text with the code to get in the gate and our site number. We didn't have to go into reception at all, which was a bonus. The grounds and amenities are spotless. The sites are massive, plenty of space.
Stayed on a grass powered site. Amazing facilities. Super clean. Camp kitchen has everything you’ll need. Amazing place to stay.

Brand spanking new... Who ever designed the layout knew what they were doing. Drive thru spaces for the largest of motorhomes or caravans. Very pleasant and helpful staff and a gate keypad that you can get to when you are driving a bus sized motorhome.
Well worth a stay. Minutes from everything.
Great stay.
Our cabin was called shingle back, orange juice tasted like chemicle and bleach andbit felt like a prison cell. If the vacanberra prison filled up this might actually be the second option
We stayed in the caravan park. We loved the drive-thru site. The amenities were modern and very clean. It was easy to get into town and to see the sites. We would thoroughly recommend it.
Stayed in motor home. New facilities with easy access into & out of sites mainly drive through. Facilities very clean & vacant lot gr8 for running my dog.

Canberra park is a great place, my school went here this year and it was amazing, the only thing wrong is that bathroom doors didn’t have locks, or at least the one I was in didn’t. Overall I would definitely recommend.

Very new facility and easy to access. Great clean facilities but not a park I would stay in again, has no character or charm. Was very noisy with alot of buses arriving with children but I guess it is canberra. Great for stop over. I would highly recommend adding some trees 🌳 would really make a huge difference.
Great stop by before heading home from a trip away, easy drive through and out! Would stay again!
Great park in a great location. Nice big powered caravan sites. Drive through. Good camp Kitchen.

We stayed at a powered site for 1 night. The park is very new and amenities are amazing, showers with good pressure  clean and new facilities. The grounds are well looked after. This is a unique looking park as it is a very open landscape. No playground to my knowledge. The only downside was noisy road/planes and it was quite bright at night, too well lit for solid sleep even with blinds closed.
Very clinical
Great modern caravan park with fantastic facilities, friendly, helpful staff.

Loads of space for car and camper. Modern and clean facilities. Fantastic communal kitchen facilities and an oval for kids to kick a footy around. Helpful staff, reasonably priced, close to cbd.  Highly recommend. Thanks from FNQ!

Very clean and real  separate disabled toilet and shower
Thankyou
全水泥地，超级干净。

Staff friendly & helpful. Ample room on concrete slab on van site surrounded by lawn for our pooch. Well planned kitchen with all facilities & outdoor area with fire pit. Amenities clean & tidy. Central dump point. Close to city & sightseeing spots. Highly recommend & plan to stay there again on next visit to Canberra.
Stayed in a drive through powered site. Staff at checking were friendly, helpful and professional, site was easy to access, clean and tidy, facilities were excellent. We will return.

All the food was bad, the rooms were dusty and my pillow was half wet and the bunk beds were so creaky i couldn't go to sleep and the shower was barely a shower, it was just a concrete floor with a shower head and no lock on the door. i will never go back again👎

We found the park online and gave them a call. The receptionist on the phone was friendly and professional. In 5 minutes we turned up with all the info we needed about the park. The grounds were well kept and quiet. The bathrooms looked new and were very clean. Everything was great.
All the facilities were excellent.
